rxn_class_summary: Electrooxidative radical cascade annulation of 1,6-enynes with sulfinates to access 7- and 9-membered sulfonamide-containing bridged/fused heterocycles via sulfonyl radical addition.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of p-toluenesulfinate (TolSO2Na, 1a) generates the sulfonyl radical (Ts·); a subsequent anodic SET oxidation of the cyclized C-radical intermediate (C or F) followed by deprotonation furnishes the aromatic product.

key_intermediates.name: vinyl radical A from Ts· addition to alkyne of 1,6-enyne (R=Ph)
aryl/spiro radical C (after intramolecular cyclization to bridged system)
alkyl radical D from Ts· addition to alkene of 2 (R=H)
cyclohexadienyl radical F (after intramolecular aromatic cyclization)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes sulfinate 1a to sulfonyl radical Ts·, which chemoselectively adds to the alkyne (Path B, R=Ph) or alkene (Path A, R=H) of the 1,6-enyne; the resulting C-radical undergoes intramolecular radical cyclization (6-exo-trig or 5-exo-dig) and then aromatic cyclization, after which a second anodic SET oxidation and deprotonation yield the bridged (4a) or fused (3aa) sulfonamide product.

probe_reagents.name: (1-cyclopropylvinyl)benzene
probe_reagents.observation: Trapped Ts· to give adduct 9 in 60% yield; benzyl radical formed was oxidized without cyclopropyl ring-opening, supporting Ts· generation and fast SET oxidation of the benzyl radical.

probe_reagents.name: Preformed medium-sized ring substrate 5
probe_reagents.observation: Under standard conditions with 1a, no 4k was formed (0%), indicating that Ts· addition does not occur after formation of the medium-sized ring; Ts· must add before cyclization.

notes: Two divergent pathways are proposed depending on alkyne substitution: Path B (R=Ph, internal alkyne) — Ts· adds to the alkyne, 6-exo-trig cyclization, then intramolecular cyclization to give bridged tricyclic sulfonamide 4a (6-7-5 system not formed here, instead bridged); Path A (R=H, terminal alkyne) — Ts· adds chemoselectively to the alkene, 5-exo-dig cyclization gives vinyl radical E, intramolecular aromatic cyclization to F, SET oxidation/deprotonation affords fused 6-7-5 product 3aa. Selectivity is rationalized by different activation barriers for Ts· addition to C=C vs C≡C depending on alkyne substitution. CV was referenced (ref 19) to support sulfinate oxidation potential.
